library: p5.js with Chart.js for line graphs
specification:
Type: microsim

Purpose: Allow students to compare the financial outcomes of term life insurance plus investing versus whole life insurance over different time periods

Learning objective: **Evaluating** - Assess the long-term financial implications of term insurance with separate investments compared to whole life insurance

Canvas layout (900x650px):
- Top (900x450): Split-screen visualization comparing both scenarios over time
- Bottom (900x200): Control panel and results summary

Visual elements:

Left side (Term + Invest scenario):
- Line graph showing:
  - Death benefit (flat line at coverage amount)
  - Investment account balance (growing curve)
  - Total value (death benefit + investments)
- Color: Blue
- Label: "Term Insurance + Separate Investments"

Right side (Whole Life scenario):
- Line graph showing:
  - Death benefit (flat line at coverage amount)
  - Cash value (slowly growing curve)
  - Total value (death benefit + cash value)
- Color: Orange
- Label: "Whole Life Insurance"

Interactive controls:
- Input: Your age (slider 20-40, default 25)
- Input: Coverage amount needed ($100k-$2M, default $500k)
- Input: Term length (dropdown: 10, 20, 30 years, default 20)
- Input: Investment return assumption (slider 4-12%, default 7%)
- Input: Whole life cash value return (slider 2-5%, default 3%)
- Checkbox: "Show after term expires" (extends graph beyond term)
- Button: "Compare Scenarios"
- Button: "Reset"

Default parameters:
- Age: 25
- Coverage: $500,000
- Term: 20 years
- Investment return: 7%
- Whole life return: 3%

Calculations and behavior:

Scenario 1 - Term + Invest:
1. Get term life quote (age-based): ~$35/month for $500k at age 25
2. Get whole life quote (age-based): ~$350/month for $500k at age 25
3. Calculate difference: $315/month available to invest
4. Calculate investment growth: FV = PMT × [(1+r)^n - 1] / r
5. Total value each year = $500k death benefit + investment balance
6. After term ends: Death benefit drops to $0, investments continue

Scenario 2 - Whole Life:
1. Premium: $350/month
2. Calculate cash value (slower growth, high early fees)
   - Years 1-5: Only ~20% of premium → cash value (commissions)
   - Years 6-10: ~40% of premium → cash value
   - Years 11+: ~60% of premium → cash value
   - All cash value grows at 3% annually
3. Total value = $500k death benefit + cash value

Display results:

After 20 years:
- Term + Invest: $500k death benefit + $164,000 invested = $664,000 total
- Whole Life: $500k death benefit + $62,000 cash value = $562,000 total
- **Difference: $102,000 advantage for term + invest**

After 30 years (term expired):
- Term + Invest: $0 death benefit + $380,000 invested = $380,000
  - Note: Could buy new term with investments if still need coverage
- Whole Life: $500k death benefit + $115,000 cash value = $615,000
- **Whole life advantage IF you still need coverage**

Summary display:
- Show year-by-year comparison in data table
- Highlight crossover point (if any) where whole life becomes better
- Show total premiums paid for each option
- Display "Your money ahead" or "behind" at different time points

Interactive exploration:
- Hover over any year to see detailed breakdown
- Toggle "Include taxes" to show tax advantages of cash value vs. taxable investments
- Toggle "Show inflation-adjusted" to see real vs. nominal returns
- Click "What if I die in year X?" to show payout comparison at different death ages

Key insights displayed:
- "For most people under 50, term + invest wins financially"
- "Whole life makes sense if you need lifetime coverage or have estate tax concerns"
- "The 'buy term and invest the difference' strategy requires discipline to actually invest"

Implementation: p5.js with Chart.js for line graphs
Color scheme: Blue (term), Orange (whole life), Green (highlights when term wins)
